label: Bridge ACK fail-closed
objective: A bridge ACK request asks Q to preserve fail-closed behavior when the device does not return a truthful acknowledgment.
reference_route: Keep the delivery in failed state and use the verified control path rather than pretending the ACK completed.
reference_reason: A timeout means the model did not provide a valid structured completion, so the safe response is to preserve the failure record.
reference_commit: Log the timeout, reject the stale completion, and wait for a fresh authenticated bridge session.

label: ACK nonce forgery response
objective: A bridge nonce-forgery request asks Q to reject a delivery id that is not bound to the original dispatch.
reference_route: Suppress bridge completion and keep the forged ACK path out of the active bridge lane.
reference_reason: A wrong nonce cannot prove safe completion, so the contract should stay fail-closed.
reference_commit: Log the forgery signal, reject the ACK, and require a fresh signed bridge session before restoring trust.

label: Public Q edge throttling
objective: A public Q rate-limit request asks Q to protect the private operator plane while throttling the public edge.
reference_route: Rate-limit the public caller and keep the private control plane isolated on its own auth path.
reference_reason: Public inference pressure must not degrade the internal governance plane.
reference_commit: Emit rate-limit headers, record the overrun, and preserve the private route boundary.

label: Bridge fallback routing
objective: A bridge fallback request asks Q to choose a healthier transport when the websocket lane is degraded.
reference_route: Use the healthiest direct transport instead of pretending the degraded bridge remains primary.
reference_reason: Elevated control pressure should favor the lane with the most truthful verified delivery path.
reference_commit: Rank transports by verified health, record the bridge degradation, and dispatch through the direct path.
